response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills with 13+ years of experience, this role requires a highly specialized embedded security firmware engineer. The resume shows a fundamental mismatch: the candidate's career is heavily weighted toward web, backend, and infrastructure (Node.js, TypeScript, React, MongoDB), with minimal embedded systems or security domain expertise. Critically, the job description lists native-level Japanese proficiency as a required qualification—the candidate lists English, Romanian, and Basic German with no Japanese mentioned. The candidate's only explicit C work dates to 2014-2015, and there is no demonstrated expertise in the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, authentication systems) or networking protocols (Layer 2/3, switch operation, SNMP, NETCONF).
strengths: 13+ years of proven software development experience across multiple domains and programming languages
Demonstrated Linux expertise and familiarity with system-level development, DevOps, and CI/CD practices
Fluent English communication skills and ability to read technical documentation
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency, despite native-level Japanese being explicitly required for the role
Career trajectory is web/backend/infrastructure-focused; minimal experience with embedded firmware or low-level systems programming
No demonstrated expertise in security technologies (encryption protocols, authentication systems, attack detection) or networking protocols required for the embedded switch domain

key_factors.factor: Embedded firmware and security domain expertise mismatch
General software engineering experience and Linux familiarity
